21	@dataclasses.dataclass
22	class SamplingBatchInfo:
23	    # Basic batched sampling params
24	    temperatures: torch.Tensor
25	    top_ps: torch.Tensor
26	    top_ks: torch.Tensor
27	    min_ps: torch.Tensor
28	
29	    # Whether all requests use greedy sampling
30	    is_all_greedy: bool
31	
32	    # Whether any requests use top_p sampling
33	    need_top_p_sampling: bool
34	
35	    # Whether any requests use top_k sampling
36	    need_top_k_sampling: bool
37	
38	    # Whether any request needs min_p sampling
39	    need_min_p_sampling: bool
40	
41	    # Masking tensors for grammar-guided structured outputs
42	    vocab_size: int
43	    grammars: Optional[List] = None
44	    vocab_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
45	    apply_mask_func: Optional[Callable[[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor], None]] = None
46	
47	    # Penalizer
48	    penalizer_orchestrator: Optional[penaltylib.BatchedPenalizerOrchestrator] = None
49	    acc_linear_penalties: torch.Tensor = None  # Used in the overlap mode
50	
51	    # Whether any request has custom logit processor
52	    has_custom_logit_processor: bool = False
53	    # Custom parameters
54	    custom_params: Optional[List[Optional[Dict[str, Any]]]] = None
55	    # Custom logit processor
56	    custom_logit_processor: Optional[
57	        Dict[int, Tuple[CustomLogitProcessor, torch.Tensor]]
58	    ] = None
59	
60	    # Used for deterministic sampling
61	    sampling_seed: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
62	
63	    # Device
64	    device: str = "cuda"
65	
66	    # Handle logit bias
67	    logit_bias: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
68	
69	    @classmethod
70	    def from_schedule_batch(cls, batch: ScheduleBatch, vocab_size: int):
71	        global_server_args = get_global_server_args()
72	        enable_deterministic = global_server_args.enable_deterministic_inference
73	
74	        reqs = batch.reqs
75	        device = batch.device
76	        temperatures = torch.tensor(
77	            [r.sampling_params.temperature for r in reqs],
78	            dtype=torch.float,
79	            device=device,
80	        ).view(-1, 1)
81	        top_ps = torch.tensor(
82	            [r.sampling_params.top_p for r in reqs], dtype=torch.float, device=device
83	        )
84	        top_ks = torch.tensor(
85	            [r.sampling_params.top_k for r in reqs], dtype=torch.int32, device=device
86	        )
87	        min_ps = torch.tensor(
88	            [r.sampling_params.min_p for r in reqs], dtype=torch.float, device=device
89	        )
90	        sampling_seed = (
91	            torch.tensor(
92	                [
93	                    (
94	                        r.sampling_params.sampling_seed
95	                        if r.sampling_params.sampling_seed is not None
96	                        else 42
97	                    )
98	                    for r in reqs
99	                ],
100	                dtype=torch.int64,
101	                device=device,
102	            )
103	            if enable_deterministic
104	            else None
105	        )
106	
107	        logit_bias = None
108	        if any(r.sampling_params.logit_bias is not None for r in reqs):
109	            logit_bias = torch.zeros(len(reqs), vocab_size, device=device)
110	            for i, r in enumerate(reqs):
111	                if r.sampling_params.logit_bias is not None:
112	                    for key, value in r.sampling_params.logit_bias.items():
113	                        logit_bias[i, int(key)] = value
114	
115	        # Check if any request has custom logit processor
116	        has_custom_logit_processor = (
117	            global_server_args.enable_custom_logit_processor
118	            and any(r.custom_logit_processor for r in reqs)  # check the flag first.
119	        )  # then check the requests.
120	
121	        if has_custom_logit_processor:
122	            # Merge the same type of custom logit processors together
123	            processor_dict = {}
124	            for i, r in enumerate(reqs):
125	                if r.custom_logit_processor is None:
126	                    continue
127	                processor_str = r.custom_logit_processor
128	                if processor_str not in processor_dict:
129	                    processor_dict[processor_str] = []
130	                processor_dict[processor_str].append(i)
131	
132	            merged_custom_logit_processor = {
133	                hash(processor_str): (
134	                    # The deserialized custom logit processor object
135	                    CustomLogitProcessor.from_str(processor_str),
136	                    # The mask tensor for the requests that use this custom logit processor
137	                    torch.zeros(len(reqs), dtype=torch.bool)
138	                    .scatter_(0, torch.tensor(true_indices), True)
139	                    .to(device, non_blocking=True),
140	                )
141	                for processor_str, true_indices in processor_dict.items()
142	            }
143	            custom_params = [r.sampling_params.custom_params for r in reqs]
144	        else:
145	            merged_custom_logit_processor = None
146	            custom_params = None
147	
148	        # Each penalizers will do nothing if they evaluate themselves as not required by looking at
149	        # the sampling_params of the requests (See {_is_required()} of each penalizers). So this
150	        # should not add hefty computation overhead other than simple checks.
151	        #
152	        # While we can choose not to even create the class instances if they are not required, this
153	        # could add additional complexity to the {ScheduleBatch} class, especially we need to
154	        # handle {filter_batch()} and {merge_batch()} cases as well.
155	        penalizer_orchestrator = penaltylib.BatchedPenalizerOrchestrator(
156	            vocab_size=vocab_size,
157	            batch=batch,
158	            penalizers={
159	                penaltylib.BatchedFrequencyPenalizer,
160	                penaltylib.BatchedMinNewTokensPenalizer,
161	                penaltylib.BatchedPresencePenalizer,
162	            },
163	        )
164	
165	        ret = cls(
166	            temperatures=temperatures,
167	            top_ps=top_ps,
168	            top_ks=top_ks,
169	            min_ps=min_ps,
170	            sampling_seed=sampling_seed,
171	            is_all_greedy=all(r.sampling_params.top_k <= 1 for r in reqs),
172	            need_top_p_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.top_p != 1.0 for r in reqs),
173	            need_top_k_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.top_k != TOP_K_ALL for r in reqs),
174	            need_min_p_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.min_p > 0 for r in reqs),
175	            vocab_size=vocab_size,
176	            penalizer_orchestrator=penalizer_orchestrator,
177	            has_custom_logit_processor=has_custom_logit_processor,
178	            custom_params=custom_params,
179	            custom_logit_processor=merged_custom_logit_processor,
180	            device=device,
181	            logit_bias=logit_bias,
182	        )
183	        ret.adjusted_from_schedule_batch(batch, vocab_size)
184	        return ret
185	
186	    # placeholder for override
187	    def adjusted_from_schedule_batch(self, batch: ScheduleBatch, vocab_size: int):
188	        pass
189	
190	    # placeholder for override
191	    def adjusted_merge_batch(self, other: "SamplingBatchInfo"):
192	        pass
193	
194	    def __len__(self):
195	        return len(self.temperatures)
196	
197	    def update_regex_vocab_mask(self):
198	        if not self.grammars:
199	            self.vocab_mask = None
200	            self.apply_mask_func = None

123	@dataclass
124	class CacheEntry:
125	    value: BaseGrammarObject
126	    event: Event
127	
128	
129	class BaseGrammarBackend:
130	    def __init__(self):
131	        self.executor = ThreadPoolExecutor()
132	        self.cache: Dict[Tuple[str, str], CacheEntry] = {}
133	
134	    def _not_supported(self, key_type: str, key_string: str) -> None:
135	        logger.warning(f"Skip unsupported {key_type=}, {key_string=}")
136	
137	    def dispatch_fallback(
138	        self, key_type: str, key_string: str
139	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
140	        """
141	        This function should not be reached in any case.
142	        """
143	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid key_type: {key_type}={key_string}")
144	
145	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
146	        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
147	
148	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
149	        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
150	
151	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
152	        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
153	
154	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
155	        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
156	
157	    def _init_value_dispatch(
158	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
159	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
160	        s = time.perf_counter()
161	        key_type, key_string = key
162	        if key_type == "json":
163	            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
164	        elif key_type == "regex":
165	            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
166	        elif key_type == "ebnf":
167	            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
168	        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
169	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
170	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
171	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
172	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
173	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
174	        else:
175	            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
176	
177	        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
178	            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
179	        return grammar
180	
181	    def get_cached_or_future_value(
182	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
183	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
184	        value = self.cache.get(key)
185	        if value:
186	            copied_value = value.copy()
187	            copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
188	            return copied_value, True
189	        value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
190	        return value, False
191	
192	    def set_cache(self, key: Tuple[str, str], value: BaseGrammarObject):
193	        self.cache[key] = value
194	
195	    def reset(self):
196	        self.cache.clear()
197

199	GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}
200	
201	
202	def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
203	    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func
204	
205	
206	def create_grammar_backend(
207	    server_args: ServerArgs,
208	    tokenizer,
209	    vocab_size: int,
210	    eos_token_ids: Optional[set] = None,
211	) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
212	    name = server_args.grammar_backend
213	
214	    # Custom grammar backend has the highest priority
215	    if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:
216	        return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](
217	            server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids
218	        )
219	
220	    # Default grammar backends
221	    if name == "outlines":
222	        from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_backend import OutlinesGrammarBackend
223	
224	        grammar_backend = OutlinesGrammarBackend(
225	            tokenizer,
226	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
227	        )
228	    elif name == "xgrammar":
229	        from sglang.srt.constrained.xgrammar_backend import (
230	            TokenizerNotSupportedError,
231	            XGrammarGrammarBackend,
232	        )
233	
234	        # Convert Set[int] to List[int] if needed
235	        eos_list = list(eos_token_ids) if eos_token_ids else None
236	
237	        try:
238	            grammar_backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
239	                tokenizer,
240	                vocab_size=vocab_size,
241	                model_eos_token_ids=eos_list,
242	                any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
243	            )
244	        except TokenizerNotSupportedError as e:
245	            logger.warning(
246	                f"Grammar backend disabled because tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar: {e}. "
247	                "Falling back to grammar_backend='none'. "
248	                "Structured outputs (JSON schema, regex, EBNF) will not be available."
249	            )
250	            server_args.grammar_backend = "none"
251	            return None
252	    elif name == "llguidance":
253	        from sglang.srt.constrained.llguidance_backend import GuidanceBackend
254	
255	        grammar_backend = GuidanceBackend(
256	            tokenizer=tokenizer,
257	            any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
258	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
259	        )
260	    elif name == "none":
261	        return None
262	    else:
263	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid grammar backend: {name}")
264	
265	    if server_args.reasoning_parser and hasattr(tokenizer, "think_end_id"):
266	        from sglang.srt.constrained.reasoner_grammar_backend import (
267	            ReasonerGrammarBackend,
268	        )
269	
270	        grammar_backend = ReasonerGrammarBackend(
271	            grammar_backend, tokenizer.think_end_id
272	        )
273	
274	    return grammar_backend

250	    def filter_batch(self, keep_indices: List[int], keep_indices_device: torch.Tensor):
251	        self.penalizer_orchestrator.filter(keep_indices_device)
252	
253	        if self.has_custom_logit_processor:

256	        for item in [
257	            "temperatures",
258	            "top_ps",

261	            "sampling_seed",

263	            value = getattr(self, item, None)
264	            if value is not None:

267	        if self.logit_bias is not None:

269	
270	    def _filter_batch_custom_logit_processor(
271	        self, keep_indices: List[int], keep_indices_device: torch.Tensor
272	    ):
273	        """Filter the custom logit processor and custom params"""
274	        self.custom_logit_processor = {
275	            k: (p, mask[keep_indices_device])
276	            for k, (p, mask) in self.custom_logit_processor.items()
277	            if torch.any(
278	                mask[keep_indices_device]
279	            )  # ignore the custom logit processor whose mask is all False
280	        }
281	        self.custom_params = [self.custom_params[i] for i in keep_indices]
282	
283	        # If the custom logit processor is an empty dict, set the flag to False,
284	        # and set the custom logit processor and custom params to None.
285	        if len(self.custom_logit_processor) == 0:
286	            self.custom_logit_processor = None
287	            self.custom_params = None
288	            self.has_custom_logit_processor = False
289	
290	    @staticmethod
291	    def merge_custom_logit_processor(
292	        lhs: Optional[Dict[int, Tuple[CustomLogitProcessor, torch.Tensor]]],
293	        rhs: Optional[Dict[int, Tuple[CustomLogitProcessor, torch.Tensor]]],
294	        bs1: int,
295	        bs2: int,
296	        device: str,
297	    ):
298	        if lhs is None and rhs is None:
299	            return None
300	        lhs, rhs = lhs or {}, rhs or {}
301	
302	        keys = set(lhs.keys()).union(set(rhs.keys()))
303	        merged_dict = {}
304	
305	        for k in keys:
306	            # Get the logit processor object
307	            processor = lhs[k][0] if k in lhs else rhs[k][0]
308	            # Get and merge the mask tensors from the two dicts
309	            left_mask = (
310	                lhs[k][1]
311	                if k in lhs
312	                else torch.zeros(bs1, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
313	            )
314	            right_mask = (
315	                rhs[k][1]
316	                if k in rhs
317	                else torch.zeros(bs2, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
318	            )
319	            merged_dict[k] = (processor, torch.cat([left_mask, right_mask]))
320	
321	            assert merged_dict[k][1].shape[0] == bs1 + bs2, (
322	                f"The batch size of merged mask ({merged_dict[k][1].shape[0]}) does not match "
323	                f"the sum of the batch sizes of the two masks ({bs1 + bs2})"
324	                f"\n{left_mask=}\n{right_mask=}\n{bs1=}\n{bs2=}"
325	                f"\n{lhs=}\n{rhs=}"
326	            )
327	
328	        return merged_dict
329	
330	    def merge_batch(self, other: "SamplingBatchInfo"):
331	        self.penalizer_orchestrator.merge(other.penalizer_orchestrator)
332	
333	        # Merge the custom logit processors and custom params lists
334	        if self.has_custom_logit_processor or other.has_custom_logit_processor:
335	            # Merge the custom logit processors
336	            self.custom_logit_processor = (
337	                SamplingBatchInfo.merge_custom_logit_processor(
338	                    self.custom_logit_processor,
339	                    other.custom_logit_processor,
340	                    len(self),
341	                    len(other),
342	                    self.device,
343	                )
344	            )
345	            # Merge the custom params lists
346	            self.custom_params = self.custom_params or [None] * len(self)
347	            other.custom_params = other.custom_params or [None] * len(other)
348	            self.custom_params.extend(other.custom_params)

42	class OutlinesGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
43	    def __init__(
44	        self,
45	        guide: RegexGuide,
46	        jump_forward_map: Union[OutlinesJumpForwardMap, None],
47	    ) -> None:
48	        super().__init__()
49	        self.guide = guide
50	        self.jump_forward_map = jump_forward_map
51	        self.state = 0
52	
53	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
54	        self.state = self.guide.get_next_state(self.state, token)
55	
56	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
57	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
58	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
59	        return torch.zeros(batch_size, vocab_size, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
60	
61	    @staticmethod
62	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
63	        return vocab_mask
64	
65	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
66	        tokens = torch.tensor(
67	            self.guide.get_next_instruction(self.state).tokens, dtype=torch.int64
68	        ).to(vocab_mask.device, non_blocking=True)
69	        vocab_mask = vocab_mask[idx]
70	        vocab_mask.fill_(1)
71	        vocab_mask.scatter_(0, tokens, torch.zeros_like(tokens, dtype=torch.bool))
72	
73	    @staticmethod
74	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor):
75	        logits.masked_fill_(vocab_mask, float("-inf"))
76	
77	    def copy(self):
78	        return OutlinesGrammar(self.guide, self.jump_forward_map)
79	
80	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple]:
81	        if not self.jump_forward_map:
82	            return None
83	
84	        jump_forward_bytes = self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_byte(self.state)
85	        if jump_forward_bytes is None or len(jump_forward_bytes) <= 1:
86	            return None
87	
88	        # preprocess the jump forward string
89	        suffix_bytes = []
90	        continuation_range = range(0x80, 0xC0)
91	        cur_state = self.state
92	        while (
93	            len(jump_forward_bytes) and jump_forward_bytes[0][0] in continuation_range
94	        ):
95	            # continuation bytes
96	            byte_edge = jump_forward_bytes.pop(0)
97	            suffix_bytes.append(byte_edge[0])
98	            cur_state = byte_edge[1]
99	
100	        suffix_tokens = [f"<0x{hex(b)[2:].upper()}>" for b in suffix_bytes]
101	        suffix_ids = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids(suffix_tokens)
102	        return suffix_ids, cur_state
103	
104	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
105	        _, cur_state = helper
106	        return self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_symbol(cur_state)
107	
108	    def jump_and_retokenize(
109	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
110	    ):
111	        self.state = next_state

114	class OutlinesGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
115	    def __init__(
116	        self,
117	        tokenizer,
118	        whitespace_pattern: str | None,
119	    ):
120	        super().__init__()
121	
122	        try:
123	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
124	        except AttributeError:
125	            # FIXME: tmp fix for chatglm2 & chatglm3 (pad_token_id=0)
126	            origin_pad_token_id = tokenizer.pad_token_id
127	
128	            def fset(self, value):
129	                self._value = value
130	
131	            type(tokenizer).pad_token_id = property(
132	                fget=type(tokenizer).pad_token_id.fget, fset=fset
133	            )
134	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
135	            self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
136	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
137	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token = (
138	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token
139	            )
140	            self.outlines_tokenizer.vocabulary = (
141	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.get_vocab()
142	            )
143	        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
144	
145	    def _compile_regex(self, regex: str) -> Optional[OutlinesGrammar]:
146	        try:
147	            if hasattr(RegexGuide, "from_regex"):
148	                # outlines >= 0.1.1
149	                guide = RegexGuide.from_regex(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
150	            else:
151	                # outlines <= 0.0.46
152	                guide = RegexGuide(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
153	        except interegular.patterns.InvalidSyntax as e:
154	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex schema: {regex=}, {e=}")
155	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
156	
157	        jump_forward_map = None
158	        return OutlinesGrammar(guide, jump_forward_map)
159	
160	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str):
161	        return super().dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
162	
163	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str):
164	        return super().dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
165	
166	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str):
167	        try:
168	            regex = build_regex_from_object(
169	                key_string,
170	                whitespace_pattern=self.whitespace_pattern,
171	            )
172	        except (NotImplementedError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as e:
173	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
174	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
175	        return self._compile_regex(regex)
176	
177	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str):
178	        return self._compile_regex(key_string)
179	
180	
181	def build_regex_from_object(
182	    object: Union[str, BaseModel, Dict], whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None
183	):
184	    if isinstance(object, type(BaseModel)):
185	        schema = json.dumps(object.model_json_schema())
186	    elif isinstance(object, Dict):
187	        schema = json.dumps(object)
188	    else:
189	        schema = object
190	    return build_regex_from_schema(schema, whitespace_pattern)

22	"""
23	Store information about requests and batches.
24	
25	The following is the flow of data structures for a batch:
26	
27	ScheduleBatch -> ModelWorkerBatch -> ForwardBatch
28	
29	- ScheduleBatch is managed by `scheduler.py::Scheduler`.
30	  It contains high-level scheduling data. Most of the data is on the CPU.
31	- ModelWorkerBatch is managed by `tp_worker.py::TpModelWorker`.
32	  It is a subset of `ScheduleBatch` that only contains data related to the model forward on GPU.
33	  It will be transformed from CPU scheduler to GPU model runner.
34	- ForwardBatch is managed by `model_runner.py::ModelRunner`.
35	  It contains low-level tensor data. Most of the data consists of GPU tensors.
36	
37	TODO(lmzheng): ModelWorkerBatch seems a bit redundant and we consider removing it in the future.
38	"""
39

47	from http import HTTPStatus

108	@lru_cache(maxsize=1)
109	def sanity_check_mm_pad_shift_value(vocab_size: int) -> None:
110	    if vocab_size > MM_PAD_SHIFT_VALUE:
111	        raise ValueError(
112	            f"Model vocab_size ({vocab_size}) exceeds MM_PAD_SHIFT_VALUE ({MM_PAD_SHIFT_VALUE}). "
113	            f"MM pad_values may overlap with valid token IDs. "
114	            f"Please increase MM_PAD_SHIFT_VALUE in schedule_batch.py."
115	        )

117	
118	def _compute_pad_value(hash: int) -> int:
119	    """Compute pad value from hash."""
120	    return MM_PAD_SHIFT_VALUE + (hash % (1 << 30))

221	@dataclasses.dataclass
222	class MultimodalDataItem:
223	    """
224	    One MultimodalDataItem contains all inputs for one modality.
225	    For example, if there are 3 images and 1 audio inputs, there will be 2 MultimodalDataItem.
226	    One for images and one for audio.
227	
228	    We put the common fields first and the model-specific fields in model_specific_data.
229	    """
230	
231	    modality: Modality
232	    hash: int = None
233	    pad_value: int = None
234	    offsets: Optional[list] = None
235	
236	    format: MultimodalInputFormat = MultimodalInputFormat.NORMAL
237	
238	    # the raw features returned by processor, e.g. pixel_values or audio_features
239	    feature: Union[torch.Tensor, np.ndarray] = None
240	    # the precomputed embeddings, passed as final encoder embeddings
241	    # One and only one of the feature and precomputed_embeddings will be empty
242	    precomputed_embeddings: Optional[Union[torch.Tensor, np.ndarray]] = None
243	
244	    # Model-specific data stored in a dictionary
245	    model_specific_data: dict[str, Any] = dataclasses.field(default_factory=dict)
246	
247	    def __getattr__(self, name: str):
248	        if (
249	            "model_specific_data" in self.__dict__
250	            and name in self.__dict__["model_specific_data"]
251	        ):
252	            return self.__dict__["model_specific_data"][name]
253	        else:
254	            raise AttributeError(
255	                f"'{self.__class__.__name__}' object has no attribute '{name}'"
256	            )
257	
258	    def __setitem__(self, key: str, value: Any):
259	        if key in self.__dict__:
260	            self.__dict__[key] = value
261	        else:
262	            self.model_specific_data[key] = value
263	
264	    def set(self, key: str, value: Any):
265	        self.__setitem__(key, value)
266	
267	    @staticmethod
268	    def is_empty_list(l):
269	        if l is None:
270	            return True
271	        return len([item for item in flatten_nested_list(l) if item is not None]) == 0
272	
273	    def set_pad_value(self):
274	        """
275	        Set the pad value after first hashing the data
276	        """
277	        if self.pad_value is not None:
278	            return
279	
280	        from sglang.srt.managers.mm_utils import hash_feature
281	
282	        if envs.SGLANG_MM_SKIP_COMPUTE_HASH.get():
283	            import uuid
284	
285	            self.hash = uuid.uuid4().int
286	            self.pad_value = _compute_pad_value(self.hash)
287	            return
288	        if self.hash is None:
289	            if self.feature is not None:
290	                hashed_feature = self.feature
291	            else:
292	                hashed_feature = self.precomputed_embeddings
293	            self.hash = hash_feature(hashed_feature)
294	        assert self.hash is not None
295	        self.pad_value = _compute_pad_value(self.hash)
296	
297	    def is_modality(self, modality: Modality) -> bool:
298	        return self.modality == modality

14	"""Sampling parameters for text generation."""
15	
16	import logging
17	from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Union
18	
19	# sre_parse is deprecated in Python 3.11+, use re._parser instead
20	try:
21	    import re._parser as sre_parse
22	except ImportError:
23	    import sre_parse  # Python < 3.11
24	
25	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-6
26	TOP_K_ALL = 1 << 30
27	
28	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

30	
31	class SamplingParams:
32	    """
33	    The sampling parameters.
34	
35	    See docs/backend/sampling_params.md or
36	    https://docs.sglang.io/backend/sampling_params.html
37	    for the documentation.
38	    """
39	
40	    def __init__(
41	        self,
42	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
43	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
44	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
45	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
46	        temperature: float = 1.0,
47	        top_p: float = 1.0,
48	        top_k: int = -1,
49	        min_p: float = 0.0,
50	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
51	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
52	        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
53	        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
54	        n: int = 1,
55	        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
56	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
57	        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
58	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
59	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
60	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
61	        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
62	        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
63	        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
64	        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
65	        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
66	        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
67	    ) -> None:
68	        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
69	        self.stop_strs = stop
70	        if stop_token_ids:
71	            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
72	        else:
73	            self.stop_token_ids = None
74	        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
75	        self.temperature = temperature
76	        self.top_p = top_p
77	        self.top_k = top_k
78	        self.min_p = min_p
79	        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
80	        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
81	        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
82	        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
83	        self.regex = regex
84	        self.n = n
85	        self.json_schema = json_schema
86	        self.ebnf = ebnf
87	        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
88	        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
89	        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
90	        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
91	        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
92	        self.custom_params = custom_params
93	        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
94	        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
95	        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed
96	
97	        # Process some special cases
98	        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
99	            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
100	            self.temperature = 1.0
101	            self.top_k = 1
102	        if self.top_k == -1:
103	            self.top_k = TOP_K_ALL  # whole vocabulary
104	
105	    def verify(self, vocab_size):
106	        if self.temperature < 0.0:
107	            raise ValueError(
108	                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}."
109	            )
110	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
111	            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
112	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
113	            raise ValueError(f"min_p must be in [0, 1], got {self.min_p}.")
114	        if self.top_k < 1 or self.top_k == -1:
115	            raise ValueError(
116	                f"top_k must be -1 (disable) or at least 1, got {self.top_k}."
117	            )
118	        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
119	            raise ValueError(
120	                "frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
121	                f"{self.frequency_penalty}."
122	            )
123	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
124	            raise ValueError(
125	                "presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got " f"{self.presence_penalty}."
126	            )
127	        if not 0.0 <= self.repetition_penalty <= 2.0:
128	            raise ValueError(
129	                "repetition_penalty must be in [0, 2], got "
130	                f"{self.repetition_penalty}."
131	            )
132	        if not 0 <= self.min_new_tokens:
133	            raise ValueError(
134	                f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens], got "
135	                f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
136	            )
137	        if self.max_new_tokens is not None:
138	            if self.max_new_tokens < 0:
139	                raise ValueError(
140	                    f"max_new_tokens must be at least 0, got {self.max_new_tokens}."
141	                )
142	            if not self.min_new_tokens <= self.max_new_tokens:
143	                raise ValueError(
144	                    f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens({self.max_new_tokens})], got "
145	                    f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
146	                )
147	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
148	            for token_id in self.logit_bias:
149	                if not 0 <= int(token_id) < vocab_size:
150	                    raise ValueError(
151	                        f"logit_bias must has keys in [0, {vocab_size - 1}], got "
152	                        f"{token_id}."
153	                    )
154	
155	        grammars = [
156	            self.json_schema,
157	            self.regex,
158	            self.ebnf,
159	        ]  # since mutually exclusive, only one can be set
160	        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
161	            raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")
162	
163	    def normalize(self, tokenizer):
164	        # Process stop strings
165	        if self.stop_strs is None:
166	            self.stop_strs = []
167	            self.stop_str_max_len = 0
168	        else:
169	            if isinstance(self.stop_strs, str):
170	                self.stop_strs = [self.stop_strs]
171	
172	            stop_str_max_len = 0
173	            for stop_str in self.stop_strs:
174	                if tokenizer is not None:
175	                    stop_str_ids = tokenizer.encode(stop_str, add_special_tokens=False)
176	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str_ids))
177	                else:
178	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str))
179	            self.stop_str_max_len = stop_str_max_len
180	
181	        # Process stop regex strings
182	        if self.stop_regex_strs is None:
183	            self.stop_regex_strs = []
184	            self.stop_regex_max_len = 0
185	        else:
186	            if isinstance(self.stop_regex_strs, str):
187	                self.stop_regex_strs = [self.stop_regex_strs]
188	
189	            stop_regex_max_len = 0
190	            for stop_regex in self.stop_regex_strs:
191	                stop_regex_max_len = max(
192	                    stop_regex_max_len, get_max_seq_length(stop_regex)
193	                )
194	
195	            self.stop_regex_max_len = stop_regex_max_len
196	
197	
198	# This function gets a strict upperbound on the maximum number of tokens that would need
199	# to be buffered to match the input regex string
200	# NOTE: in the worst case, one character that needs to be buffered corresponds to one
201	# token
202	def get_max_seq_length(regex_str: str):
203	    return _max_length_from_subpattern(sre_parse.parse(regex_str))
204	
205	
206	MAX_LEN = 2**30
207	
208	
209	def _max_length_from_subpattern(subpattern: sre_parse.SubPattern):
210	    total = 0
211	    for token, value in subpattern:
212	        if token in {
213	            sre_parse.LITERAL,  # `value` is any one character
214	            sre_parse.IN,  # Any character within `value`
215	            sre_parse.ANY,  # "."
216	        }:
217	            total += 1
218	        elif token == sre_parse.SUBPATTERN:
219	            # EG: (a\d+) ->
220	            # [(SUBPATTERN,
221	            #   (1, 0, 0, [(LITERAL, 97),
222	            #              (MAX_REPEAT, (1, MAXREPEAT, [(IN, [(CATEGORY, CATEGORY_DIGIT)])]))]))]
223	            _, _, _, inner_subpattern = value
224	            total += _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
225	        elif token == sre_parse.BRANCH:
226	            _, branches = value
227	            total += max(_max_length_from_subpattern(branch) for branch in branches)
228	        elif token in {sre_parse.MAX_REPEAT, sre_parse.MIN_REPEAT}:
229	            _, max_num_repeat, inner_subpattern = value
230	            if max_num_repeat == sre_parse.MAXREPEAT:
231	                total += MAX_LEN
232	            else:
233	                total += max_num_repeat * _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
234	        elif token == sre_parse.AT:
235	            # These are zero-width assertions like ^, $, and \b that don't add to the max
236	            # length
237	            total += 0
238	        else:
239	            logger.warning(f"Got unhandled regex token: {token}")
240	
241	            total += MAX_LEN
242	
243	    return total

31	class SamplingParams:
32	    """
33	    The sampling parameters.
34	
35	    See docs/backend/sampling_params.md or
36	    https://docs.sglang.io/backend/sampling_params.html
37	    for the documentation.
38	    """
39	
40	    def __init__(
41	        self,
42	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
43	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
44	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
45	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
46	        temperature: float = 1.0,
47	        top_p: float = 1.0,
48	        top_k: int = -1,
49	        min_p: float = 0.0,
50	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
51	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
52	        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
53	        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
54	        n: int = 1,
55	        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
56	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
57	        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
58	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
59	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
60	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
61	        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
62	        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
63	        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
64	        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
65	        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
66	        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
67	    ) -> None:
68	        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
69	        self.stop_strs = stop
70	        if stop_token_ids:
71	            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
72	        else:
73	            self.stop_token_ids = None
74	        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
75	        self.temperature = temperature
76	        self.top_p = top_p
77	        self.top_k = top_k
78	        self.min_p = min_p
79	        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
80	        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
81	        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
82	        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
83	        self.regex = regex
84	        self.n = n
85	        self.json_schema = json_schema
86	        self.ebnf = ebnf
87	        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
88	        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
89	        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
90	        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
91	        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
92	        self.custom_params = custom_params
93	        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
94	        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
95	        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed
96	
97	        # Process some special cases
98	        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
99	            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
100	            self.temperature = 1.0
101	            self.top_k = 1
102	        if self.top_k == -1:
103	            self.top_k = TOP_K_ALL  # whole vocabulary

24	class TestJsonSchemaConstraint(unittest.TestCase):
25	    """Test JSON schema constraint generation for tool choices"""
26	
27	    def setUp(self):
28	        """Set up test tools"""
29	        self.tools = [
30	            Tool(
31	                type="function",
32	                function=Function(
33	                    name="get_weather",
34	                    description="Get weather information",
35	                    parameters={
36	                        "type": "object",
37	                        "properties": {
38	                            "location": {
39	                                "type": "string",
40	                                "description": "Location to get weather for",
41	                            },
42	                            "unit": {
43	                                "type": "string",
44	                                "enum": ["celsius", "fahrenheit"],
45	                                "description": "Temperature unit",
46	                            },
47	                        },
48	                        "required": ["location"],
49	                    },
50	                ),
51	            ),
52	            Tool(
53	                type="function",
54	                function=Function(
55	                    name="search",
56	                    description="Search for information",
57	                    parameters={
58	                        "type": "object",
59	                        "properties": {
60	                            "query": {
61	                                "type": "string",
62	                                "description": "Search query",
63	                            },
64	                        },
65	                        "required": ["query"],
66	                    },
67	                ),
68	            ),
69	        ]
70	
71	    def test_required_tool_choice_schema(self):
72	        """Test schema generation for tool_choice='required'"""
73	        schema = get_json_schema_constraint(self.tools, "required")
74	
75	        self.assertIsNotNone(schema)
76	        jsonschema.Draft202012Validator.check_schema(schema)
77	
78	        self.assertEqual(schema["type"], "array")
79	        self.assertEqual(schema["minItems"], 1)
80	        self.assertIn("items", schema)
81	        self.assertIn("anyOf", schema["items"])
82	
83	        # Should have schemas for both tools
84	        self.assertEqual(len(schema["items"]["anyOf"]), 2)
85	
86	        # Check that each tool schema is present
87	        tool_names = [
88	            item["properties"]["name"]["enum"][0] for item in schema["items"]["anyOf"]
89	        ]
90	        self.assertIn("get_weather", tool_names)
91	        self.assertIn("search", tool_names)
92	
93	    def test_specific_tool_choice_schema(self):
94	        """Test schema generation for specific tool choice"""
95	        tool_choice = ToolChoice(
96	            type="function", function=ToolChoiceFuncName(name="get_weather")
97	        )
98	        schema = get_json_schema_constraint(self.tools, tool_choice)
99	
100	        self.assertIsNotNone(schema)

41	class SchedulerOutputProcessorMixin:
42	    """
43	    This class implements the output processing logic for Scheduler.
44	    We put them into a separate file to make the `scheduler.py` shorter.
45	    """
46	
47	    def _get_storage_backend_type(self) -> str:
48	        """Get storage backend type from tree_cache."""
49	        storage_backend_type = "none"
50	        cache_controller = getattr(self.tree_cache, "cache_controller", None)
51	        if cache_controller and hasattr(cache_controller, "storage_backend"):
52	            storage_backend = cache_controller.storage_backend
53	            if storage_backend is not None:
54	                storage_backend_type = type(storage_backend).__name__
55	        return storage_backend_type
56	
57	    def _get_cached_tokens_details(self, req: Req) -> Optional[dict]:
58	        """Get detailed cache breakdown for a request, if available.
59	
60	        Returns:
61	            - None if HiCache is not enabled
62	            - {"device": X, "host": Y} if HiCache enabled but L3 storage is not
63	            - {"device": X, "host": Y, "storage": Z, "storage_backend": "..."} if L3 enabled
64	        """
65	        # Only show details if HiCache is enabled
66	        if not getattr(self, "enable_hierarchical_cache", False):
67	            return None
68	
69	        # Only show if there are any cached tokens
70	        if (
71	            req.cached_tokens_device > 0
72	            or req.cached_tokens_host > 0
73	            or req.cached_tokens_storage > 0
74	        ):
75	            details = {
76	                "device": req.cached_tokens_device,
77	                "host": req.cached_tokens_host,
78	            }
79	            # Only include storage fields if L3 storage is enabled
80	            if getattr(self, "enable_hicache_storage", False):
81	                details["storage"] = req.cached_tokens_storage
82	                details["storage_backend"] = self._get_storage_backend_type()
83	            return details
84	        return None
85	
86	    def process_batch_result_prebuilt(self: Scheduler, batch: ScheduleBatch):
87	        assert self.disaggregation_mode == DisaggregationMode.DECODE
88	        for req in batch.reqs:
89	            req.check_finished()
90	            if req.finished():
91	                req.time_stats.forward_entry_time = req.time_stats.completion_time = (
92	                    time.perf_counter()
93	                )
94	                trace_slice_end(
95	                    RequestStage.DECODE_QUICK_FINISH,
96	                    req.rid,
97	                    thread_finish_flag=True,
98	                )
99	                release_kv_cache(req, self.tree_cache)
100	
101	        # Note: Logprobs should be handled on the prefill engine.
102	        trace_slice_batch(RequestStage.DECODE_FAKE_OUTPUT, batch.reqs)
103	        self.stream_output(batch.reqs, batch.return_logprob)
104	
105	    def maybe_collect_routed_experts(self: Scheduler, req: Req):
106	        """Collect routed experts for a finished request."""
107	        req.routed_experts = get_global_experts_capturer().get_routed_experts(
108	            req_pool_idx=req.req_pool_idx,
109	            seqlen=req.seqlen,
110	            req_to_token_pool=self.req_to_token_pool,
111	        )
112	
113	    def maybe_collect_customized_info(
114	        self: Scheduler, i: int, req: Req, logits_output: LogitsProcessorOutput
115	    ):
116	        if logits_output is not None and logits_output.customized_info is not None:
117	            if req.customized_info is None:
118	                req.customized_info = {}
119	            for k, v in logits_output.customized_info.items():
120	                if k not in req.customized_info:
121	                    req.customized_info[k] = []
122	                req.customized_info[k].append(v[i])
123	
124	    def process_batch_result_prefill(
125	        self: Scheduler,
126	        batch: ScheduleBatch,
127	        result: Union[GenerationBatchResult, EmbeddingBatchResult],
128	    ):
129	        skip_stream_req = None
130	
131	        if self.is_generation:
132	            if result.copy_done is not None:
133	                result.copy_done.synchronize()
134	
135	            (
136	                logits_output,
137	                next_token_ids,
138	                extend_input_len_per_req,
139	                extend_logprob_start_len_per_req,
140	            ) = (
141	                result.logits_output,
142	                result.next_token_ids,
143	                result.extend_input_len_per_req,
144	                result.extend_logprob_start_len_per_req,
145	            )
146	
147	            # Move next_token_ids and logprobs to cpu
148	            next_token_ids = next_token_ids.tolist()
149	            if batch.return_logprob:
150	                if logits_output.next_token_logprobs is not None:
151	                    logits_output.next_token_logprobs = (
152	                        logits_output.next_token_logprobs.tolist()
153	                    )
154	                if logits_output.input_token_logprobs is not None:
155	                    logits_output.input_token_logprobs = tuple(
156	                        logits_output.input_token_logprobs.tolist()
157	                    )
158	
159	            hidden_state_offset = 0
160	
161	            # Check finish conditions
162	            logprob_pt = 0
163	
164	            for i, (req, next_token_id) in enumerate(zip(batch.reqs, next_token_ids)):
165	                if req.finished() or req.is_retracted:
166	                    # decode req in mixed batch or retracted req
167	                    continue
168	
169	                if req.is_chunked <= 0:
170	                    if req.time_stats.prefill_finished_ts == 0.0:
171	                        req.time_stats.prefill_finished_ts = time.time()
172	
173	                    # req output_ids are set here
174	                    req.output_ids.append(next_token_id)
175	                    req.check_finished()
176	
177	                    if req.finished():
178	                        self.maybe_collect_routed_experts(req)
179	                        release_kv_cache(req, self.tree_cache)
180	                        req.time_stats.completion_time = time.perf_counter()
181	                    elif not batch.decoding_reqs or req not in batch.decoding_reqs:
182	                        # This updates radix so others can match
183	                        self.tree_cache.cache_unfinished_req(req)
184	
185	                    self.maybe_collect_customized_info(i, req, logits_output)
186	
187	                    if batch.return_logprob:
188	                        assert extend_logprob_start_len_per_req is not None
189	                        assert extend_input_len_per_req is not None
190	                        extend_logprob_start_len = extend_logprob_start_len_per_req[i]
191	                        extend_input_len = extend_input_len_per_req[i]
192	
193	                        num_input_logprobs = self._calculate_num_input_logprobs(
194	                            req, extend_input_len, extend_logprob_start_len
195	                        )
196	
197	                        if req.return_logprob:
198	                            self.add_logprob_return_values(
199	                                i,
200	                                req,
201	                                logprob_pt,
202	                                next_token_ids,
203	                                num_input_logprobs,
204	                                logits_output,
205	                            )
206	                        logprob_pt += num_input_logprobs
207	
208	                    if (
209	                        req.return_hidden_states
210	                        and logits_output.hidden_states is not None
211	                    ):
212	                        req.hidden_states.append(
213	                            logits_output.hidden_states[
214	                                hidden_state_offset : (
215	                                    hidden_state_offset := hidden_state_offset
216	                                    + len(req.origin_input_ids)
217	                                )
218	                            ]
219	                            .cpu()
220	                            .clone()
221	                            .tolist()
222	                        )
223	
224	                    if req.grammar is not None:
225	                        # FIXME: this try-except block is for handling unexpected xgrammar issue.
226	                        try:
227	                            req.grammar.accept_token(next_token_id)
228	                        except ValueError as e:
229	                            # Grammar accept_token can raise ValueError if the token is not in the grammar.
230	                            # This can happen if the grammar is not set correctly or the token is invalid.
231	                            logger.error(
232	                                f"Grammar accept_token failed for req {req.rid} with token {next_token_id}: {e}"
233	                            )
234	                            self.abort_request(AbortReq(rid=req.rid))
235	                        req.grammar.finished = req.finished()
236	
237	                    trace_slice(
238	                        RequestStage.PREFILL_FORWARD,
239	                        req.rid,
240	                        auto_next_anon=not req.finished(),
241	                        thread_finish_flag=req.finished(),
242	                    )
243	
244	                else:
245	                    # being chunked reqs' prefill is not finished
246	                    req.is_chunked -= 1
247	                    # There is only at most one request being currently chunked.
248	                    # Because this request does not finish prefill,
249	                    # we don't want to stream the request currently being chunked.
250	                    skip_stream_req = req
251	
252	                    # Incrementally update input logprobs.
253	                    if batch.return_logprob:
254	                        extend_logprob_start_len = extend_logprob_start_len_per_req[i]
255	                        extend_input_len = extend_input_len_per_req[i]

257	                            # Update input logprobs.
258	                            num_input_logprobs = self._calculate_num_input_logprobs(
259	                                req, extend_input_len, extend_logprob_start_len
260	                            )
261	                            if req.return_logprob:
262	                                self.add_input_logprob_return_values(
263	                                    i,
264	                                    req,
265	                                    logits_output,
266	                                    logprob_pt,
267	                                    num_input_logprobs,

272	                    trace_slice(

275	                        auto_next_anon=True,
276	                    )
277	
278	        else:  # embedding or reward model

337	    def _resolve_spec_overlap_token_ids(
338	        self: Scheduler, result: GenerationBatchResult, batch: ScheduleBatch
339	    ) -> List[List[int]]:
340	        """Resolve the padding next token ids for speculative decoding with overlap."""
341	        assert result.next_token_ids.is_cpu
342	        assert result.accept_lens.is_cpu
343	
344	        next_token_ids = result.next_token_ids.tolist()
345	        accept_lens = result.accept_lens.tolist()
346	        result.num_accepted_tokens = sum(accept_lens) - len(batch.reqs)
347	        result.accept_length_per_req_cpu = [x - 1 for x in accept_lens]
348	
349	        predict_tokens = []
350	        stride = self.draft_worker.speculative_num_draft_tokens
351	
352	        for i, req in enumerate(batch.reqs):
353	            req.kv_committed_len += accept_lens[i]
354	            predict_tokens.append(
355	                next_token_ids[i * stride : i * stride + accept_lens[i]]
356	            )
357	            req.spec_verify_ct += 1
358	
359	            accepted_draft_tokens = result.accept_length_per_req_cpu[i]
360	            req.spec_accepted_tokens += accepted_draft_tokens
361	            req.update_spec_acceptance_histogram(accepted_draft_tokens)
362	
363	        return predict_tokens

540	    def _mamba_prefix_cache_update(
541	        self, req: Req, batch: ScheduleBatch, result: GenerationBatchResult, i: int
542	    ) -> None:
543	        seq_len = len(req.origin_input_ids) + len(req.output_ids) - 1
544	        if req.mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer is not None:
545	            mamba_track_interval = get_global_server_args().mamba_track_interval
546	            if batch.spec_algorithm.is_none() and seq_len % mamba_track_interval == 0:
547	                # for non-spec decode, we update mamba_last_track_seqlen at the end of each track interval
548	                req.mamba_next_track_idx = 1 - req.mamba_next_track_idx
549	                req.mamba_last_track_seqlen = seq_len
550	            elif (
551	                not batch.spec_algorithm.is_none()
552	                and result.accept_length_per_req_cpu is not None
553	            ):
554	                # for spec decode, update mamba_last_track_seqlen if this iteration crosses a track interval
555	                actual_seq_len = req.seqlen - 1
556	                if (
557	                    actual_seq_len // mamba_track_interval
558	                    != (actual_seq_len - result.accept_length_per_req_cpu[i])
559	                    // mamba_track_interval
560	                ):
561	                    req.mamba_last_track_seqlen = (
562	                        actual_seq_len // mamba_track_interval * mamba_track_interval
563	                    )

570	
571	        # Process logprob values - handle multi-item scoring vs regular requests
572	        if is_multi_item_scoring:
573	            # Multi-item scoring: use all logprobs as-is
574	            req.input_token_logprobs_val = input_token_logprobs
575	        else:
576	            # Regular request: add None at start, remove last (sampling token)
577	            req.input_token_logprobs_val = [None] + input_token_logprobs[:-1]
578	
579	        # Process logprob indices based on scoring type
580	        if is_multi_item_scoring:
581	            # Multi-item scoring: only include delimiter token positions
582	            relevant_tokens = req.origin_input_ids[req.logprob_start_len :]

584	                token_id

586	                if token_id == self.server_args.multi_item_scoring_delimiter
587	            ]
588	        else:
589	            # Regular request: include all tokens from logprob_start_len onwards
590	            input_token_logprobs_idx = req.origin_input_ids[req.logprob_start_len :]
591	
592	        # Clip padded hash values from image tokens to prevent detokenization errors
593	        req.input_token_logprobs_idx = [
594	            x if x < self.model_config.vocab_size - 1 else 0
595	            for x in input_token_logprobs_idx
596	        ]

57	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
58	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
59	        return torch.zeros(batch_size, vocab_size, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)

61	    @staticmethod
62	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:

66	        tokens = torch.tensor(
67	            self.guide.get_next_instruction(self.state).tokens, dtype=torch.int64
68	        ).to(vocab_mask.device, non_blocking=True)
69	        vocab_mask = vocab_mask[idx]

77	    def copy(self):

80	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple]:
81	        if not self.jump_forward_map:
82	            return None
83	
84	        jump_forward_bytes = self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_byte(self.state)
85	        if jump_forward_bytes is None or len(jump_forward_bytes) <= 1:
86	            return None
87	
88	        # preprocess the jump forward string
89	        suffix_bytes = []
90	        continuation_range = range(0x80, 0xC0)
91	        cur_state = self.state
92	        while (
93	            len(jump_forward_bytes) and jump_forward_bytes[0][0] in continuation_range
94	        ):
95	            # continuation bytes
96	            byte_edge = jump_forward_bytes.pop(0)
97	            suffix_bytes.append(byte_edge[0])
98	            cur_state = byte_edge[1]
99	
100	        suffix_tokens = [f"<0x{hex(b)[2:].upper()}>" for b in suffix_bytes]
101	        suffix_ids = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids(suffix_tokens)
102	        return suffix_ids, cur_state

104	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
105	        _, cur_state = helper
106	        return self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_symbol(cur_state)
107	
108	    def jump_and_retokenize(
109	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
110	    ):
111	        self.state = next_state

114	class OutlinesGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
115	    def __init__(
116	        self,
117	        tokenizer,
118	        whitespace_pattern: str | None,
119	    ):
120	        super().__init__()
121	
122	        try:
123	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
124	        except AttributeError:
125	            # FIXME: tmp fix for chatglm2 & chatglm3 (pad_token_id=0)
126	            origin_pad_token_id = tokenizer.pad_token_id
127	
128	            def fset(self, value):
129	                self._value = value
130	
131	            type(tokenizer).pad_token_id = property(
132	                fget=type(tokenizer).pad_token_id.fget, fset=fset
133	            )
134	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
135	            self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
136	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
137	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token = (
138	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token
139	            )
140	            self.outlines_tokenizer.vocabulary = (
141	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.get_vocab()
142	            )
143	        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
144	
145	    def _compile_regex(self, regex: str) -> Optional[OutlinesGrammar]:
146	        try:
147	            if hasattr(RegexGuide, "from_regex"):
148	                # outlines >= 0.1.1
149	                guide = RegexGuide.from_regex(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
150	            else:
151	                # outlines <= 0.0.46
152	                guide = RegexGuide(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
153	        except interegular.patterns.InvalidSyntax as e:
154	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex schema: {regex=}, {e=}")
155	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
156	
157	        jump_forward_map = None
158	        return OutlinesGrammar(guide, jump_forward_map)
159	
160	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str):
161	        return super().dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
162	
163	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str):
164	        return super().dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
165	
166	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str):
167	        try:
168	            regex = build_regex_from_object(
169	                key_string,
170	                whitespace_pattern=self.whitespace_pattern,
171	            )
172	        except (NotImplementedError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as e:
173	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
174	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
175	        return self._compile_regex(regex)
176	
177	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str):
178	        return self._compile_regex(key_string)
179	
180	
181	def build_regex_from_object(
182	    object: Union[str, BaseModel, Dict], whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None
183	):
184	    if isinstance(object, type(BaseModel)):
185	        schema = json.dumps(object.model_json_schema())
186	    elif isinstance(object, Dict):
187	        schema = json.dumps(object)
188	    else:
189	        schema = object
190	    return build_regex_from_schema(schema, whitespace_pattern)

356	class Runtime:
357	    """
358	    A wrapper for the HTTP server.
359	    This is used for launching the server in a python program without
360	    using the command line interface.
361	
362	    It is mainly used for the frontend language.
363	    You should use the Engine class if you want to do normal offline processing without the frontend language.
364	    """
365	
366	    def __init__(
367	        self,
368	        log_level: str = "error",
369	        launch_timeout: float = 300.0,
370	        *args,
371	        **kwargs,
372	    ):
373	        """See the arguments in server_args.py::ServerArgs
374	
375	        Args:
376	            log_level: Log level for the server.
377	            timeout: Timeout in seconds for waiting for the server to start.
378	            *args: Additional arguments passed to ServerArgs.
379	            **kwargs: Additional keyword arguments passed to ServerArgs.
380	        """
381	        # We delay the import of any `sglang.srt` components in `sglang.lang`, so users can run
382	        # client code without installing SRT server and its dependency if they want.
383	        from sglang.srt.entrypoints.http_server import launch_server
384	        from sglang.srt.server_args import ServerArgs
385	        from sglang.srt.utils import is_port_available
386	
387	        self.server_args = ServerArgs(*args, log_level=log_level, **kwargs)
388	
389	        # Pre-allocate ports
390	        for port in range(self.server_args.port, 40000):
391	            if is_port_available(port):
392	                break
393	        self.server_args.port = port
394	
395	        self.url = self.server_args.url()
396	        self.generate_url = self.url + "/generate"
397	
398	        # NOTE: We store pid instead of proc to fix some issues during __delete__
399	        self.pid = None
400	
401	        ctx = multiprocessing.get_context("spawn")
402	        proc = ctx.Process(
403	            target=launch_server,
404	            args=(self.server_args,),
405	        )
406	        proc.start()
407	        self.pid = proc.pid
408	
409	        # Before python program terminates, call shutdown implicitly. Therefore, users don't have to explicitly call .shutdown()
410	        atexit.register(self.shutdown)
411	
412	        # Wait for server to be ready by polling /health_generate
413	        start_time = time.time()
414	        with requests.Session() as session:
415	            while time.time() - start_time < launch_timeout:
416	                try:
417	                    response = session.get(f"{self.url}/health_generate")
418	                    if response.status_code == 200:
419	                        break
420	                except requests.RequestException:
421	                    pass
422	
423	                if not proc.is_alive():
424	                    self.shutdown()
425	                    raise RuntimeError(
426	                        "Initialization failed. Please see the error messages above."
427	                    )
428	
429	                time.sleep(2)
430	            else:
431	                self.shutdown()
432	                raise TimeoutError("Server failed to start within the timeout period.")
433	
434	        self.endpoint = RuntimeEndpoint(self.url)
435	
436	    def shutdown(self):
437	        from sglang.srt.utils import kill_process_tree
438	
439	        if self.pid is not None:
440	            kill_process_tree(self.pid)
441	            self.pid = None
442	
443	    def start_profile(self):
444	        self.endpoint.start_profile()
445	
446	    def stop_profile(self):
447	        self.endpoint.stop_profile()
448	
449	    def cache_prefix(self, prefix: str):
450	        self.endpoint.cache_prefix(prefix)
451	
452	    def get_tokenizer(self):
453	        from sglang.srt.utils.hf_transformers_utils import get_tokenizer

2	from dataclasses import dataclass

8	@dataclass
9	class ChoicesDecision:
10	    decision: str
11	    meta_info: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None

13	
14	class ChoicesSamplingMethod(ABC):
15	
16	    @property
17	    def requires_unconditional_logprobs(self) -> bool:
18	        return False
19	
20	    @abstractmethod
21	    def __call__(
22	        self,
23	        *,
24	        choices: List[str],
25	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
26	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
27	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
28	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
29	    ) -> ChoicesDecision: ...

32	class TokenLengthNormalized(ChoicesSamplingMethod):
33	
34	    def __call__(
35	        self,
36	        *,
37	        choices: List[str],
38	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
39	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
40	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
41	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
42	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
43	        """Select the option with the highest token length normalized prompt logprob."""
44	        best_choice = choices[np.argmax(normalized_prompt_logprobs)]
45	        meta_info = {
46	            "normalized_prompt_logprobs": normalized_prompt_logprobs,
47	            "input_token_logprobs": input_token_logprobs,
48	            "output_token_logprobs": output_token_logprobs,
49	        }
50	        return ChoicesDecision(decision=best_choice, meta_info=meta_info)

53	token_length_normalized = TokenLengthNormalized()

55	
56	class GreedyTokenSelection(ChoicesSamplingMethod):
57	
58	    def __call__(
59	        self,
60	        *,
61	        choices: List[str],
62	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
63	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
64	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
65	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
66	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
67	        """Select the option based on greedy logprob selection. For overlapping options
68	        where one option is a subset of a longer option, extend the shorter option using
69	        its average logprob for comparison against the longer option."""
70	
71	        num_options = len(choices)
72	        max_tokens = max(len(option) for option in input_token_logprobs)
73	        logprob_matrix = self._build_logprob_matrix(
74	            input_token_logprobs, max_tokens, num_options
75	        )
76	        remaining = self._greedy_selection(logprob_matrix, num_options, max_tokens)
77	
78	        best_choice = choices[remaining[0]]
79	        meta_info = {
80	            "normalized_prompt_logprobs": normalized_prompt_logprobs,
81	            "input_token_logprobs": input_token_logprobs,
82	            "output_token_logprobs": output_token_logprobs,
83	            "greedy_logprob_matrix": logprob_matrix.tolist(),
84	        }
85	        return ChoicesDecision(decision=best_choice, meta_info=meta_info)
86	
87	    def _build_logprob_matrix(self, input_token_logprobs, max_tokens, num_options):
88	        logprob_matrix = np.zeros((num_options, max_tokens))
89	        for i, option in enumerate(input_token_logprobs):
90	            actual_logprobs = [token[0] for token in option]
91	            avg_logprob = np.mean(actual_logprobs)
92	            logprob_matrix[i, : len(option)] = actual_logprobs
93	            if len(option) < max_tokens:
94	                logprob_matrix[i, len(option) :] = avg_logprob
95	        return logprob_matrix
96	
97	    def _greedy_selection(self, logprob_matrix, num_options, max_tokens):
98	        remaining = np.arange(num_options)
99	        for j in range(max_tokens):
100	            max_logprob = np.max(logprob_matrix[remaining, j])
101	            remaining = remaining[logprob_matrix[remaining, j] == max_logprob]
102	            if len(remaining) == 1:
103	                break
104	        return remaining
105	
106	
107	greedy_token_selection = GreedyTokenSelection()
108	
109	
110	class UnconditionalLikelihoodNormalized(ChoicesSamplingMethod):
111	
112	    @property
113	    def requires_unconditional_logprobs(self) -> bool:
114	        return True
115	
116	    def __call__(
117	        self,
118	        *,
119	        choices: List[str],
120	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
121	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
122	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
123	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
124	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
125	        """Select the option with the highest average token logprob once normalized by
126	        the unconditional token logprobs.
127	
128	        The first unconditional token logprob is assumed to be None. If so, it is
129	        replaced with 0 for the purposes of normalization."""
130	
131	        if unconditional_token_logprobs is None:
132	            raise ValueError(
133	                "Unconditional token logprobs are required for this method."
134	            )
135	
136	        normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs = self._normalize_logprobs(
137	            input_token_logprobs, unconditional_token_logprobs
138	        )
139	
140	        best_choice = choices[np.argmax(normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs)]
141	        meta_info = {
142	            "normalized_prompt_logprobs": normalized_prompt_logprobs,
143	            "input_token_logprobs": input_token_logprobs,
144	            "output_token_logprobs": output_token_logprobs,
145	            "unconditional_token_logprobs": unconditional_token_logprobs,
146	            "normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs": normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs,
147	        }
148	        return ChoicesDecision(decision=best_choice, meta_info=meta_info)
149	
150	    def _normalize_logprobs(self, input_token_logprobs, unconditional_token_logprobs):
151	        normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs = []
152	        for inputs, unconditionals in zip(
153	            input_token_logprobs, unconditional_token_logprobs
154	        ):
155	            inputs_logprobs = np.array([token[0] for token in inputs])
156	            unconditionals_logprobs = np.array([token[0] for token in unconditionals])
157	            unconditionals_logprobs[0] = unconditionals_logprobs[0] or 0
158	            normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs.append(
159	                float(np.mean(inputs_logprobs - unconditionals_logprobs))
160	            )
161	        return normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs
162	
163	
164	unconditional_likelihood_normalized = UnconditionalLikelihoodNormalized()

40	class Sampler(nn.Module):
41	    def __init__(self):
42	        super().__init__()
43	        self.use_nan_detection = get_global_server_args().enable_nan_detection
44	        self.tp_sync_group = get_tp_group().device_group
45	        if is_dp_attention_enabled():
46	            self.tp_sync_group = get_attention_tp_group().device_group
47	
48	        self.rl_on_policy_target = get_global_server_args().rl_on_policy_target
49	        # In RL on-policy mode, deterministic inference is automatically enabled.
50	        self.enable_deterministic = (
51	            get_global_server_args().enable_deterministic_inference
52	        )
53	        # In RL on-policy mode, we use log_softmax to compute logprobs to match the trainer.
54	        self.use_log_softmax_logprob = self.rl_on_policy_target is not None
55	        self.use_ascend_backend = get_global_server_args().sampling_backend == "ascend"

57	    def _preprocess_logits(
58	        self, logits: torch.Tensor, sampling_info: SamplingBatchInfo
59	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
60	        """Apply custom logit processors and handle NaN detection."""
61	        # Apply the custom logit processors if registered in the sampling info
62	        if sampling_info.has_custom_logit_processor:
63	            apply_custom_logit_processor(logits, sampling_info)
64	
65	        # Detect and handle NaN values in logits
66	        if self.use_nan_detection and torch.any(torch.isnan(logits)):
67	            logger.warning("Detected errors during sampling! NaN in the logits.")
68	            logits = torch.where(
69	                torch.isnan(logits), torch.full_like(logits, -1e5), logits
70	            )
71	            if crash_on_warnings():
72	                raise ValueError("Detected errors during sampling! NaN in the logits.")
73	
74	        return logits

76	    def forward(
77	        self,
78	        logits_output: LogitsProcessorOutput,

82	        token_ids_logprobs: List[List[int]],
83	        positions: torch.Tensor,
84	    ):
85	        """Run a sampler & compute logprobs and update logits_output accordingly.
86	
87	        Args:
88	            logits_output: The logits from the model forward
89	            sampling_info: Metadata for sampling
90	            return_logprob: If set, store the output logprob information to
91	                logits_output
92	            top_logprobs_nums: Number of top lobprobs per sequence in a batch
93	            token_ids_logprobs: Per-sequence list of specific token IDs to retrieve
94	                logprobs for. Each element is a list of token IDs (or None) for one
95	                sequence in the batch. This is used in speculative decoding.
96	            positions: The positions of the tokens in the sequence. Used for deterministic sampling
97	                to get the unique seed for each position.
98	        """
99	        logits = logits_output.next_token_logits
100	
101	        # Preprocess logits (custom processors and NaN handling)
102	        logits = self._preprocess_logits(logits, sampling_info)
103	
104	        if sampling_info.is_all_greedy:
105	            # Use torch.argmax if all requests use greedy sampling
106	            batch_next_token_ids = torch.argmax(logits, -1)
107	            if return_logprob:

109	                    logits, dim=-1
110	                )

122	            # In RL on-policy mode, we use log_softmax to compute logprobs to match the trainer.

132	                del logits_div_temperature

134	            if self.use_ascend_backend:
